title: Lake Como Boat Rental Guide: Costs, Seasons and Tips for 2026
html:
Renting a boat on Lake Como opens up the dramatic shoreline of the western Alps. From the port of Como a half-day motorboat costs from €250 in June, and boat rental Lake Como can be booked through BoatHire24. Explore the water on your own terms with verified listings.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Lake Como?

Prices range from €150 for a shared tour to €3,500 for a crewed luxury yacht per day. The exact cost depends on boat type, season and duration. For a half-day self-drive motorboat you should budget €250-€700. Full-day catamarans typically cost €800-€1,800. Crewed yachts with skipper run €1,500-€3,500. Check the boat rental Lake Como listings on BoatHire24 for live availability. You can boat rental Lake Como for your chosen dates.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Lake Como?

Most rental boats under 40 hp do not require a licence, but the operator confirms exact requirements. In Italy a patente nautica is needed for boats over 40 hp or with a length exceeding 10 metres. Rental companies on Lake Como typically ask for a credit-card deposit and a brief safety briefing. Always verify with the BoatHire24 lister before booking.

Where to set off in Lake Como

Lake Como has dozens of public and private piers. The main departure points include Como town, Bellagio, Menaggio and Varenna. These three central towns form the popular 'golden triangle' with regular ferry connections. Other good starting points are Lezzeno, Lenno (near Villa del Balbianello) and Colico at the northern tip. For a quieter launch try Laglio or Cernobbio. Many renters begin at the marina in Domaso or the port in Gravedona. One option worth a look is the Fairline Targa 43 listing.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (e.g. Limpopo), best for couples or small groups exploring the coast; 4-5 guests; from €250 half-day.
  2. Day cruiser (e.g. Fairline Targa 43), ideal for a full-day family outing; 6-8 guests; from €1,200 day; check the Fairline Targa 43 listing on BoatHire24.
  3. Sailing yacht (e.g. Lagoon 40), perfect for a relaxing day on the water; 8-10 guests; from €1,500 full-day.
  4. Luxury motor yacht (e.g. Azimut 50), suits corporate events or special celebrations; 10-12 guests; from €2,500 crewed.
  5. Electric boat, silent and eco-friendly for a romantic sunset trip; 6 guests; from €180 half-day.

Book early for July and August when demand peaks and prices rise by up to 30 percent. The 'tivano' morning wind often picks up after 10 am, so beginners should start early.

Costs and seasons at a glance

  • Price band: €150 (shared tour) to €3,500 (crewed yacht) per day
  • Peak months: July, August, September (book 4-6 weeks ahead)
  • Typical trip length: half-day (4 hours) or full-day (8 hours)
  • Capacity: 2 to 12 passengers depending on boat
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Lake Como

Can I rent a boat in Lake Como without a licence?

Yes, many rental boats under 40 hp are available without a licence, but confirm with the provider.

What is the best time of year for boating on Lake Como?

The best season runs from April to October, with July and August offering the warmest water.

How far is Bellagio from Como by boat?

The distance is approximately 15 nautical miles, taking about 45 minutes at cruising speed.

Are there guided tours available if I prefer not to drive?

Yes, many operators offer skippered charters and shared group tours starting from €150 per person.

A self-drive rental on Lake Como gives you the freedom to explore hidden coves and historic villas at your own pace. Check the full inventory on BoatHire24 to compare boats, prices and availability. Boat owners can also list their vessel on the platform to reach thousands of travellers.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Lake Como and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
